australian_relation: National Trust NSW public institutional article with first-hand and historically framed supernatural accounts attached to named house museums.
coordinate_evidence_note: National Trust NSW page names Woodford Academy and describes the Lady in White / Mary James ghost tradition; coordinates use the state-checked OSM/Nominatim Woodford Academy point.
evidence_summary: National Trust NSW says Woodford Academy is the oldest complex of colonial buildings in the Blue Mountains and that local myth suggests the house may be visited by two female ghosts. The first is known as the Lady in White, identified as Mary James. The page says Mary James' legacy has evolved into that of a troubled spirit who lingers around Woodford Academy, occasionally appearing in the attic window and frightening visitors.
publication_or_organisation: Spooky stories at the National Trust; National Trust NSW Editor
title: Lady in White in Woodford Academy Lady in White

australian_relation: National Trust NSW public institutional article with first-hand and historically framed supernatural accounts attached to named house museums.
coordinate_evidence_note: National Trust NSW page names Woodford Academy and separately describes the Lady in Black / Caroline ghost tradition; coordinates use the state-checked OSM/Nominatim Woodford Academy point.
evidence_summary: National Trust NSW reports that another local myth around Woodford Academy concerns Mary James' daughter Caroline, known as the Lady in Black. The page connects Caroline's tragic local history with reports that the Lady in White and the Lady in Black are ghosts at the Academy, while staff discuss curtains twitching in the attic and whether the ghosts are quiet because their stories are being told.
publication_or_organisation: Spooky stories at the National Trust; National Trust NSW Editor
title: Lady in Black in Woodford Academy Lady in Black

australian_relation: National Trust NSW public institutional article with first-hand and historically framed supernatural accounts attached to named house museums.
coordinate_evidence_note: National Trust NSW page names Old Government House in Parramatta Park and describes a woman in period costume / Mary Bligh apparition account; coordinates use the state-checked OSM/Nominatim Old Government House point.
evidence_summary: National Trust NSW says Old Government House has spooky stories including footsteps, cigar smoke, and multiple sightings of a woman in period costume standing at the bottom of the grand staircase. The page says staff have spotted a woman on the second floor wearing a long blue dress; when asked her name, she said Mary, as in Mary Bligh, and said she was looking after the house.
publication_or_organisation: Spooky stories at the National Trust; National Trust NSW Editor
title: Mary in Old Government House Mary apparition

australian_relation: National Trust NSW public institutional article with first-hand and historically framed supernatural accounts attached to named house museums.
coordinate_evidence_note: National Trust NSW page names Saumarez Homestead in Armidale and identifies the purported ghost as Elsie White; coordinates use the state-checked OSM/Nominatim Saumarez Homestead point.
evidence_summary: National Trust NSW asks whether Saumarez Homestead is haunted and quotes Sally-Anne Prado describing curious moments when the upstairs occupant made its presence felt. The page says the ghost likes to sit on beds, occasionally plays a few piano keys, and is purported to be Elsie White, who spent almost all of her 97 years at Saumarez Homestead in Armidale.
publication_or_organisation: Spooky stories at the National Trust; National Trust NSW Editor
title: Elsie White in Saumarez Homestead Elsie White ghost
